description: submit_npu_inference calls dma_sync_single_for_device() between the host memcpy and the npu_enqueue, ensuring the input buffer is coherent for the NPU's DMA engine. submit_cpu_log_block and submit_cpu_trace_block submit to NPU ops that only the CPU reads back, where cache coherency isn't required. Unifying onto a shared submit_block() that drops the cache sync corrupts every NPU inference whose input contains recently-written data.

findings.rationale: submit_npu_inference has an extra dma_sync_single_for_device() call between the memcpy and the npu_enqueue; the other two submit straight to the queue. The cache-sync is load-bearing on the non-coherent NPU path. extra-call shape, on the critical path leading to npu_enqueue.
